aiEngine.title: AI Engine
aiEngine.resetCliDescription: Resets Claude/Codex CLI paths to defaults and invalidates engine caches. Useful for testing the first-launch detection flow. If no CLI is found on PATH, you'll return to the initial connection screen.
aiEngine.resetCliConfirm: This resets Claude/Codex CLI paths to defaults and triggers re-detection. If the CLI isn't on PATH, the app returns to the initial connection screen. Continue?

plugins.description: Manage Polaris built-in and discovered plugin UI entries and MCP capabilities. Changes are saved locally immediately.
plugins.coreLocked: The core plugin owns base panel entries and cannot be disabled in this version.

systemPrompt.enableDesc: Customize the system prompt sent to AI
systemPrompt.modeAppendHint: Append custom content after the default prompt
systemPrompt.modeReplaceHint: Completely replace the default prompt with custom content
systemPrompt.placeholder:
Enter your custom system prompt...

Available variables:
{{workspaceName}} - Current workspace name
{{workspacePath}} - Current workspace path
{{contextWorkspaces}} - Context workspaces list
{{date}} - Current date
{{time}} - Current time
{{defaultPrompt}} - Default system prompt
systemPrompt.resetConfirm: Are you sure you want to reset to default settings?
systemPrompt.variablesHint: Click a variable to insert it into the editor:

autoMode.description: Auto mode controls which actions Claude can perform without user confirmation
autoMode.descriptionDetail: Allow rules are executed automatically, deny rules require confirmation. These rules protect your system and data.
autoMode.customRulesEmpty: No custom rules yet, click above to add

lsp.description: Manage language servers (LSP) for code completion, diagnostics, go-to-definition, find references, and more.
lsp.helpTitle: How to add a language server (3 steps)
lsp.helpStep1: Install the server first (Polaris does not bundle it). The presets below include install commands — click one to fill the form.
lsp.helpStep2: languages must use the internal language id (lowercase): use cpp for C++, csharp for C#.
lsp.helpStep3: Open a file of that language to trigger the connection; a green dot means success, red means you can click Restart.
lsp.helpPresetsTitle: Common servers and how to install
lsp.helpLowSpec: Java's jdtls starts a JVM and resides at hundreds of MB. On low-spec machines, use Index mode instead: word-scan based go-to-definition and find references, zero resident process, no memory cost (no completion/diagnostics).
lsp.helpShortcuts: Default shortcuts: Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+B go to definition · Alt+Shift+R find references · Ctrl/Cmd+Click go to definition (kept) · Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+O document symbols. Customize via 'LSP Shortcuts' above.

shortcuts.title: LSP Shortcuts
shortcuts.hint: Click a button and press your combo to record. Reopen files for changes to take effect.
shortcuts.note: Note: F12 is reserved (DevTools toggle); Ctrl/Cmd+Click go-to-definition is always kept.

lsp.modeLspHint: Starts a language server process; provides completion/diagnostics/semantic navigation.
lsp.modeIndexHint: No install, zero resident process; provides go-to/find-references, no completion/diagnostics.
lsp.checkNotFound: Executable not found. Please install it and ensure it is on PATH.
lsp.formatOnSaveHint: Calls LSP textDocument/formatting before Ctrl/Cmd+S; silently skipped when no LSP.

web.applyDesc: Save and click 'Apply' to take effect immediately — no restart needed
web.applyFailed: Failed to apply, please try again
web.accessHintDesc: Save Web settings to take effect immediately. Open the access URL in a browser to use directly.
web.actualPortHint: Preferred port is in use; currently using {{port}}
web.qrHint: Scan the QR code with your phone to access the Polaris web interface
web.qrNoIp: No LAN IP detected. Please check your network connection.
web.qrDisabled: QR code will appear after enabling Web service
web.connectionLostDesc: Real-time updates unavailable. Please check if the desktop app is running.
web.tokenHint: Empty = no check. When set, every Web API request will include this token (MD5 on frontend before sending).

appUpdate.title: App Update
appUpdate.description: Check GitHub Releases for MSI updates and view historical versions.
appUpdate.upToDateDesc: You are already using the latest version.
appUpdate.historyDesc: View published GitHub Releases and download MSI installers.
appUpdate.rollbackWarning: Rollback installs an older MSI and may require uninstalling the current version first. Back up user configuration before downgrading.

personalHub.supabaseDesc: Enter your Supabase project URL and anon key for sign-in/sign-up and data sync. The anon key is public; row-level security (RLS) keeps data isolated.
personalHub.encryptionDesc: Used for AES encryption of the description field in the links table. Stored in the local config file — keep it safe; losing it means previously encrypted content cannot be decrypted. Use a sufficiently long random string.
personalHub.encryptionPlaceholder: Enter or generate an encryption key
personalHub.usage1: Fill in the Supabase configuration and click "Save" at the bottom
personalHub.usage2: Click the "Personal Hub" icon in the left ActivityBar to sign in
personalHub.usage3: After signing in, manage navigation, bookmarks and todos, with optional encryption
personalHub.guideIntro: A public demo project is preconfigured by default. To use your own Supabase project (independent data, fully customizable), follow these steps:
personalHub.step1Desc: Sign up at Supabase and create a new project. Note the region and database password.
personalHub.step2Desc: Go to Authentication → Providers → Email and ensure Email/Password login is enabled.
personalHub.step2Note: Optional: during development, disable "Confirm email" under Authentication → Settings so you can sign in immediately after registering.
personalHub.step3Desc: Go to SQL Editor → New query, paste the script below and run it. It creates the links table, indexes, an updated_at trigger, and RLS row-level security policies. The script is idempotent and safe to re-run.
personalHub.step3Note: Note: RLS policies restrict each row to its owner (auth.uid() = user_id). The anon key is safe to use with RLS; no service_role key is needed.
personalHub.step4Title: Get the Project URL and anon key
personalHub.step4Desc: Go to Project Settings → API and copy the "Project URL" and the "anon public" key.
personalHub.step5Title: Fill in the config above and save
personalHub.step5Desc: Enter the URL and anon key in the inputs at the top of this page and click "Save". Optionally configure an encryption key to encrypt sensitive descriptions.
personalHub.security1: The anon key is public; combined with RLS policies, leaking it does not grant unauthorized access.
personalHub.security2: Never use the service_role key on the client — it bypasses all RLS.
personalHub.security3: The encryption key is stored only in the local config file; losing it means previously encrypted content cannot be decrypted.
